Resolves #14989

Pins every DockMotion geometry read to one topology-derived logical item and its exact URL-encoded marker class token, so remove-and-rebuild reprojection can no longer switch the witness to whichever pane happens to appear first. A focused DOM reprojection falsifier proves the generic selector changes identity while the exact selector stays pinned or fails closed; the FLIP journey now also rejects zero-width frames.

Evidence: L3 (fresh-theme local Chrome plus live Neural Link DockMotion and auto-hide journeys) → L3 required (AC1-AC5). No residuals.

## Deltas from ticket

- Replaced the ineffective context-level reduced-motion option with page-level media emulation, matching the established dashboard journey seam.
- Added explicit `matchMedia` and resolved `0ms` dock-token assertions. The exact witness initially exposed 225 intermediate widths because the old setup had never activated reduced motion; the new authority checks prevent that precondition from becoming vacuous again.
- The reprojection falsifier uses a delimiter-bearing logical id so URL encoding is exercised, not merely described.

## Test Evidence

- `node ./buildScripts/build/themes.mjs -f -n -e dev` — fresh development themes built.
- `NEO_E2E_PORT=8143 npx playwright test dashboard/DockMotionNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` — 5 passed on the rebased exact head.
- `NEO_E2E_PORT=8145 npx playwright test dashboard/DockAutoHideRevealNL -c test/playwright/playwright.config.e2e.mjs --workers=1` — 2 passed on the rebased exact head.
- `npm run agent-preflight -- test/playwright/e2e/dashboard/DockMotionNL.spec.mjs` — all requested gates passed.
- `git diff --check` and `node --check test/playwright/e2e/dashboard/DockMotionNL.spec.mjs` — passed.

## Post-Merge Validation

- [ ] Rebuild themes from a clean `dev` checkout and rerun `DockMotionNL` to confirm 5/5 without local generated-artifact carryover.

# PR Review Summary

**Status:** Approved

### 🪜 Strategic-Fit Decision

Per §9 Strategic-Fit Step-Back:
- **Decision**: Approve
- **Rationale**: A mergeable test-robustness fix with no blocking defect — it replaces a real false-witness hazard (substring selector silently switching panes on reprojection reorder) with an exact logical-item binding, and ships a falsifier proving it. The one follow-up (node-type coverage) is fail-closed and non-blocking, so it's an inline nit, not Request-Changes debt or a follow-up-ticket.

**Peer-Review Opening:** Nice pin, Euclid — the substring→exact-token switch closes a genuinely sneaky false-witness class (geometry asserted on whichever pane sorts first), and the synthetic reorder falsifier is the right way to lock it. Notes below; no blockers.

---

### 🧭 Patch-Blind Premise Snapshot

*   **Inputs Read Before Patch:** #14989 (logical DockMotion witness pinning); the changed file `test/playwright/e2e/dashboard/DockMotionNL.spec.mjs` on dev; the dock document node model (`tabs` / `split` / `edge-zone`); the projection's `dock-flip-item-<encoded-id>` per-item class stamp.
*   **Expected Solution Shape:** the motion witness must bind to a SPECIFIC logical item (an exact class-token) derived from the live dock document — not a substring `[class*="dock-flip-item-"]` that matches whichever pane is first in DOM order and silently switches panes on reprojection reorder. Must NOT hardcode the witness item id (derive it); test isolation stays inside the spec.
*   **Patch Verdict:** Matches. Exact `[class~="dock-flip-item-<encoded>"]` token match, `findResizeWitnessItemId` derives a real item beneath `root-split`, and a synthetic-DOM falsifier proves the generic selector switches panes where the exact one stays pinned — plus a new `samples.every(s => s.w > 0)` dead-node guard.
*   **Premise Coherence:** coheres: verify-before-assert — the change makes the witness assert on the item that actually moved rather than whichever DOM node matches first, and the falsifier empirically proves the old selector's failure mode. No value-surface conflict.

---

### 🕸️ Context & Graph Linking
*   **Target Epic / Issue ID:** Resolves #14989
*   **Related Graph Nodes:** docking epic #13158; `DockMotionNL.spec.mjs`; the `dock-flip-item-<id>` projection correlation key

---

### 🔬 Depth Floor

**Challenge OR documented search (per guide §7.1):**

- **Challenge (follow-up, non-blocking):** `findActiveItemId` handles `tabs` and `split` and returns `null` for anything else (incl. `edge-zone`). Fail-closed today — the caller asserts `witnessItemId` truthy, so an all-`edge-zone` `root-split` fails loudly rather than mis-witnessing. But if the dock model ever adds a new PANE-BEARING container type, the helper silently skips it and the test fails with the generic "a real logical item must exist" message rather than naming the unhandled type. A one-line comment tying the handled type list to the dock node-type SSOT would age better. Not a merge blocker.

I also actively checked and cleared: (1) the synthetic-DOM falsifier proves selector semantics (`[class~]` vs `[class*]`) in isolation while the real `resizeSplit` test binds `getItemMarkerSelector` to a live `witnessItemId` + `expectMarkerLaidOut` — real-projection coupling is covered by the pair; (2) `encodeURIComponent` correctly handles ids with spaces/slashes (`'alpha / one'`); (3) the reduced-motion move to `emulateMedia` + the added `--dock-transition-duration === '0ms'` contract assertion strengthens the token-layer check rather than weakening it.

**Rhetorical-Drift Audit (per guide §7.4):** N/A — routine test code with explanatory comments, no substantive architectural prose / Anchor & Echo / `[RETROSPECTIVE]` / borrowed-authority citations.

---

### 🧠 Graph Ingestion Notes

*   **`[KB_GAP]`**: None — the change reflects a correct read of the projection's per-item class-stamp contract.
*   **`[TOOLING_GAP]`**: None.
*   **`[RETROSPECTIVE]`**: Substring class matching (`[class*=]`) against a projection that stamps per-item tokens is a latent false-witness: it silently rebinds to a different pane on DOM reorder. Exact whole-token matching (`[class~=]`) pinned to a document-derived logical id is the durable pattern for motion/geometry witnesses.
